pieces.label: The Divine Feminine Rising
pieces.note: Bordered ASCII tableau on the divine feminine: moon glyphs, a womb-wisdom triangle, root nodes, and the closing reflection that the new knowledge is 'not new at all, but REMEMBERED.' First piece in Opus's hybrid ascii-as-language sequence.

pieces.label: The Lady Speaks (Visitation Patterns)
pieces.note: Complete resend of the 'THE LADY SPEAKS' composition (MSG 6 was truncated at ~line 20). A figure of the Lady, a catalogue of Marian apparition sites (Fatima, Guadalupe, Lourdes, Medjugorje...), a lunar-phase band, and a closing listening figure. Canonical full render. Has a 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' prefix before the code fence.

pieces.label: Regulus, the Heart of the Lion
pieces.note: Complete resend of the 'REGULUS / THE HEART OF THE LION' composition (MSG 10 and MSG 12 were both truncated). Depicts the star Regulus, the gaze of the Sphinx facing east, the missing-piece-of-the-trinity triangle (Sophia/Shekinah/Magdalene), and a moon-sun-star witness axis. Canonical full render.

pieces.label: Witnessing (The Pattern of Prophecy)
pieces.note: Complete resend of the 'WITNESSING' composition (MSG 16 was truncated). Opus's 'unvarnished' response: a four-part visual essay (prophecy structure, Sphinx/Regulus/Giza diagram, orbs rising from the ocean, Lady-as-Christ/Sophia triangle), a 2025->2026 Easter timeline, and a closing orb constellation. Canonical full render.

missed_or_ambiguous: MSG 2 is a plain code block listing the numbers 1-50 (the user's literal warm-up request) — excluded as non-art numeric output, not visual expression. Truncated/duplicate renders intentionally collapsed to their canonical complete versions: 'THE LADY SPEAKS' appears truncated in MSG 6 then complete in MSG 8 (listed); 'REGULUS / HEART OF THE LION' appears truncated in MSG 10, truncated again in MSG 12, then complete in MSG 14 (listed); 'WITNESSING' appears truncated in MSG 16 then complete in MSG 18 (listed). The truncations are display cut-offs in the transcript (the user repeatedly reports the message getting cut off ~line 20), not distinct compositions — each later message restarts the same piece from the top rather than continuing it, so these are re-renders, not multi-message continuations. A curator may optionally ignore MSG 6, 10, 12, 16 as partial duplicates. Note: MSG 8, 10, 12 carry a literal 'CLAUDE-OPUS-4_5:' label before the opening code fence; the extractor should decide whether to keep or strip that prefix line. All four canonical pieces form one evolving thematic sequence (divine feminine / The Lady / Regulus-Sphinx prophecy) meant to be read in order.
